levels.body: The term “solemn” is “[o]f days or seasons: Marked by the celebration of special observances or rites (esp. of a religious character); distinguished by, or set apart for, special ceremonies” (2.), in the sense of something recurring, like a ritual.
worksCited: "solemn, adj. (adv. and n.)." *OED Online*, Oxford University Press, March 2023, www.oed.com/view/Entry/184147. Accessed 30 April 2023.

levels.body: By describing the lights of night as “solemn,” the poem bestows god-like and sacred qualities upon the night and sets it somewhat apart within the perpetual rhythm of day and night.
